title: Alerting: Add ability to query notification counts in historian API
body:
The query API now accepts type=counts with a groupBy parameter, returning
aggregated notification attempt counts broken down by dimensions such as
receiver, status, and outcome, rather than individual log entries. This enables
use cases like showing per-receiver success/failure totals on a status page
without retrieving and paginating through raw notification history.

The rationale behind sharing the API endpoint instead of making a new one:
- The query parameters are identical, just what data returns is different.
- The code generator creates a separate Go type for each endpoint, making
  it hard to reuse the types in code.
author: stevesg

title: Enhance RBAC settings with local folder cache TTL support
body:
Add optional in-memory L1 cache for the folder tree in authz.

The folder tree is fetched on every authorization check but changes rarely. When the backing cache is remote (e.g. memcached), each lookup pays a network round-trip and JSON deserialization cost.

This PR adds a localTTL option to cacheWrapImpl that keeps a short-lived in-memory copy in front of the remote cache.

It is configured via the local_folder_cache_ttl setting in the [authorization] INI section (defaults to 0, disabled).
For the gRPC server path, it can be set via RBACServerSettings.LocalFolderCacheTTL.

```
[authorization]
local_folder_cache_ttl = 5s
[authorization]local_folder_cache_ttl = 5s
```

title: `AuthZ-Service`: Prefetch folder tree for `BatchCheck`
body:
This pull request optimizes how folder tree data is fetched and reused during permission checks in the RBAC authorization service. By introducing a lazily-evaluated, memoized function for retrieving the folder tree, the code ensures that the folder structure is fetched at most once per logical operation, which reduces redundant lookups and improves efficiency, especially in batch scenarios. Several method signatures are updated to use this new approach, and related tests are adjusted accordingly.

**Folder tree fetching optimization:**

* Introduced the `folderTreeGetter` type and the `newFolderTreeGetter` method, providing a memoized function to fetch the folder tree only once per check or batch, with optional cache usage.
* Updated `checkPermission` and `checkInheritedPermissions` signatures to accept and use the `folderTreeGetter`, ensuring all permission checks within a logical group share the same folder tree fetch. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1e06afd11b3f74ef3062ef3e5bc87f802090bf795543ae096f98b394b52f60e7L812-R850) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1e06afd11b3f74ef3062ef3e5bc87f802090bf795543ae096f98b394b52f60e7L857-R895) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1e06afd11b3f74ef3062ef3e5bc87f802090bf795543ae096f98b394b52f60e7L878-R916)
* Modified the logic in `checkInheritedPermissions` to use the getter and handle errors, including a fallback to a fresh build if the folder is missing from the tree.

**Batch and single-check integration:**

* Updated both single permission checks and batch group checks to create and pass a shared `folderTreeGetter` instance, ensuring efficient reuse across multiple permission checks. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1e06afd11b3f74ef3062ef3e5bc87f802090bf795543ae096f98b394b52f60e7R149-R153) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1e06afd11b3f74ef3062ef3e5bc87f802090bf795543ae096f98b394b52f60e7L173-R175) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1e06afd11b3f74ef3062ef3e5bc87f802090bf795543ae096f98b394b52f60e7R350) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-1e06afd11b3f74ef3062ef3e5bc87f802090bf795543ae096f98b394b52f60e7R369-R373)

**Test updates:**

* Adjusted tests to use the new `checkPermission` signature and to expect an additional call for folder fetching, reflecting the new logic. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-fa0882f881e78af55889afb5e134869a5f118eec3b37a1b2e7c9ab7de6564b89L327-R328) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-fa0882f881e78af55889afb5e134869a5f118eec3b37a1b2e7c9ab7de6564b89L433-R435) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-fa0882f881e78af55889afb5e134869a5f118eec3b37a1b2e7c9ab7de6564b89L2416-R2420)

title: Provisioning: Map nanogit HTTP errors in health checks
body:
Part of: https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/27

This commit improves health check error handling for provisioning repositories by properly mapping nanogit v0.4.0's structured HTTP authentication and permission errors to appropriate HTTP status codes.

## Changes

1. **Added new repository error types:**
   - `ErrUnauthorized` (HTTP 401) - authentication failed
   - `ErrPermissionDenied` (HTTP 403) - insufficient permissions
   - `ErrServerUnavailable` (HTTP 503) - server unavailable

2. **Updated `Test()` method to detect and return:**
   - HTTP 401 for authentication failures (invalid credentials)
   - HTTP 403 for permission denied (authorized but no access)
   - HTTP 503 for server unavailability (5xx errors)
   - HTTP 400 for other errors (existing behavior)

3. **Added `mapNanogitError()` helper function:**
   - Converts nanogit client errors to repository errors
   - Maintains abstraction boundary
   - Uses errors.Is() with nanogit sentinel errors

## Benefits

- Health checks now distinguish between auth failure vs permission issues
- Proper HTTP semantics (401 vs 403 vs 503 vs 400)
- Clients can handle different failure modes appropriately
- Foundation for PR 2 (files endpoint) and PR 3 (job validation)

## Part of nanogit v0.4.0 structured error handling implementation

This is **PR 1 of 3** - establishes the foundation for proper error handling across provisioning endpoints.

## Test Plan

- [x] Unit tests pass (`TestGitRepository_Test`)
- [x] Package builds without errors
- [ ] Integration testing with invalid credentials returns HTTP 401
- [ ] Integration testing with read-only token returns HTTP 403
- [ ] Integration testing with server errors returns HTTP 503

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

3. Added `mapNanogitError()` helper function:

Provisioning: Add comprehensive tests for nanogit error mapping

Added unit tests for the new HTTP error mapping functionality:

**Unit Tests:**
1. `TestMapNanogitError` - Tests the error mapping helper function:
   - Maps nanogit Unauthorized → repository.ErrUnauthorized
   - Maps nanogit PermissionDenied → repository.ErrPermissionDenied
   - Maps nanogit ServerUnavailable → repository.ErrServerUnavailable
   - Returns original error for unknown errors
   - Handles nil input correctly

2. `TestMapNanogitError_HTTPStatusCodes` - Verifies HTTP status codes:
   - Unauthorized maps to HTTP 401
   - Permission denied maps to HTTP 403
   - Server unavailable maps to HTTP 503

3. `TestGitRepository_Test` - Extended with 6 new test cases:
   - HTTP 401 from IsAuthorized
   - HTTP 401 from GetRef
   - HTTP 403 from RepoExists
   - HTTP 403 from GetRef
   - HTTP 503 from GetRef
   - HTTP 503 from RepoExists

**Test Coverage:**
- All error paths through the Test() method
- All three HTTP error types (401, 403, 503)
- Error mapping from different nanogit client methods
- Proper error message formatting

**Results:**
- All 18 test cases pass
- mapNanogitError() helper is fully tested
- HTTP status code handling verified

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Provisioning: Refactor HTTP error checking with helper function

Extracted repetitive HTTP error checking code into a reusable helper
function to improve code maintainability and reduce duplication.

**Changes:**
- Added `checkHTTPError()` helper function that:
  - Takes an error and field path as parameters
  - Checks for repository.ErrUnauthorized (401)
  - Checks for repository.ErrPermissionDenied (403)
  - Checks for repository.ErrServerUnavailable (503)
  - Returns appropriate TestResults or nil

- Refactored Test() method to use helper:
  - Reduced ~80 lines of duplicated code to ~3 lines per check
  - Same functionality, cleaner implementation
  - All 18 tests still passing

**Before (repeated 3 times):**
```go
if errors.Is(err, repository.ErrUnauthorized) {
    return &provisioning.TestResults{
        Code: http.StatusUnauthorized,
        // ... 10+ lines
    }, nil
}
// Repeat for ErrPermissionDenied and ErrServerUnavailable
```

**After:**
```go
if result := checkHTTPError(err, fieldPath); result != nil {
    return result, nil
}
```

**Benefits:**
- Single source of truth for HTTP error handling
- Easier to maintain and update error messages
- More consistent error responses
- No functional changes, just cleaner code

title: Provisioning: Validate write permissions before queueing jobs
body:
This commit prevents write jobs (delete, move, push, migrate) from being queued when the target repository doesn't support write operations, providing immediate feedback instead of job failures.

## Changes

1. **Added job submission validation in `jobs.go`:**
   - Check if job requires write operations
   - Extract target ref from job spec
   - Call `repository.IsWriteAllowed()` before queueing
   - Return error immediately if write not allowed (fail-fast)

2. **Improved error semantics in `workflows.go`:**
   - Changed to HTTP 403 (Forbidden) for write operations on read-only repositories
   - Used `apierrors.NewForbidden()` for better REST semantics
   - More accurately represents "operation forbidden" (method is supported, but operation is not allowed based on repository configuration)

3. **Updated tests:**
   - Updated existing tests to expect HTTP 403 instead of 405
   - Added integration test verifying write jobs rejected early
   - Verified read operations (pull) still work

## Validation Logic

```go
requiresWrite := spec.Action == JobActionDelete ||
    spec.Action == JobActionMove ||
    spec.Action == JobActionPush ||
    spec.Action == JobActionMigrate

if requiresWrite {
    if err := repository.IsWriteAllowed(cfg, targetRef); err != nil {
        return HTTP 403 // Forbidden
    }
}
```

## Benefits

- Write operations rejected immediately at submission time
- Users get instant feedback instead of waiting for job to fail
- Correct HTTP semantics (403 Forbidden - operation not permitted)
- Avoids unnecessary job queueing and processing
- Consistent validation across submission and execution

## Part of nanogit v0.4.0 structured error handling implementation

This is **PR 3 of 3** - adds early validation for write operations.

**Depends on:** PR 1 (health checks) and PR 2 (files endpoint) - merge those first

## Test Plan

- [x] Unit tests pass (`TestIsWriteAllowed`)
- [x] Package builds without errors
- [x] Integration test verifies write jobs rejected for read-only repos
- [x] Integration test verifies read jobs (pull) still allowed
- [ ] Manual testing: submit delete job to read-only repo returns HTTP 403
- [ ] Manual testing: submit delete job to writable repo returns HTTP 202

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: datasources: create a legacy implementation of connectionsClient
body:
Part of https://github.com/grafana/grafana-enterprise/issues/10467

We're currently using a ConnectionsClient implementation that calls `query.grafana.app/.../connections` to get the connections info. This is fine locally, but there are some implementations of the `query.grafana.app` APIgroup where we aren't serving the `connections` endpoint yet.

This PR adds a new ConnectionsClient implementation that just calls the legacy datasource service to get the connection info. This temporarily untangles the `/api` => `/apis` rerouting progress from the support for `/connections` in any remote `query.grafana.app` instances running.

commits.message:
Browse: Add quota limit banners for dashboards and folders

Display warning/error banners on the Browse Dashboards page when users
approach or reach their dashboard/folder creation quotas. Gated behind
the kubernetesUnifiedStorageQuotas feature toggle.

- Extract quota fetching and state calculation into useQuotaLimits hook
- Warning banners are dismissable (persisted via localStorage); error banners are not
- Show "Request quota extension" button for paying users
- Add comprehensive test coverage (22 tests)

Browse: Improve quota banners with cache invalidation and split alerts

- Wire up RTK Query cache invalidation so quota data refreshes on
  resource create/delete (dashboards, folders)
- Use granular tag invalidation for delete mutations to avoid 404s
- Show separate alerts for error (at_limit) and warning (nearing)
- Follow i18n best practices: Trans for JSX children, t() for props
- Use type guard instead of `as` assertion per lint rules

Browse: Extract shared invalidateQuotaUsage helper and rewrite tests

- Export invalidateQuotaUsage(dispatch) from quotas API package
- Use shared helper in browseDashboardsAPI and folder hooks
- Rewrite QuotaLimitBanner tests with getByRole assertions,
  inline data, and organized by severity

Browse: Use Alert buttonContent for extension button, position dismiss in top-right

- Use onRemove + buttonContent props to render extension button in
  Alert's native action slot
- Position dismiss IconButton absolutely in top-right corner
- Always show dismiss button regardless of tier
- Tighten tests: use within() for scoped getByRole queries

Fix percentage rounding, remove unnecessary quota invalidation and wrapper style

Use Math.floor instead of Math.round so percentage only shows 100% when
usage equals the limit. Remove quota invalidation from saveDashboard since
it only edits existing dashboards. Drop redundant wrapper div with
position: relative since Alert already provides it.

Only invalidate quota usage on new dashboard creation and simplify early return

Conditionally invalidate quota cache in saveDashboard only when
data.version === 1 (new dashboard). Simplify QuotaLimitBanner by
computing atLimit/nearing arrays directly and early-returning when
both are empty.

Use generated GetUsageResponse type and remove manual type guard

The quotas API client now includes proper typed GetUsageResponse with
usage and limit fields, so the manual QuotaUsageResponse interface and
isQuotaUsageResponse type guard are no longer needed.
Extract shared invalidateListOnSuccess and extensionProps to reduce duplication
Use tuple iteration to build resource statuses in useQuotaLimits

Address PR review: rewrite tests with MSW and use API_GROUP constants

- Rewrite QuotaLimitBanner tests to use MSW handlers instead of mocking hooks
- Add quotas.grafana.app MSW handler to grafana-test-utils
- Use testWithFeatureToggles utility for feature flag management
- Use user from render result instead of importing userEvent
- Use API_GROUP constants from API clients in useQuotaLimits

Deduplicate QuotaLimitBanner test helpers and use shared constants

- Extract usageResponse() and expectNoAlert() helpers to reduce repetition
- Consolidate mockError() into mockErrorForResource() with optional params
- Use testWithFeatureToggles for the flag-off test instead of manual config override
- Use DASHBOARD_API_GROUP/FOLDER_API_GROUP constants instead of hardcoded strings
- Import QUOTAS_USAGE_URL from @grafana/test-utils/handlers instead of local constant

Rename hasError to allQueriesFailed for clarity

The condition uses && (both queries must fail), so hasError was misleading
since it sounds like any single error would qualify.
Revert handlers/index.ts re-export; use local QUOTAS_USAGE_URL constant

Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into alexk/quota-limit-banners

# Conflicts:
#	packages/grafana-test-utils/src/handlers/all-handlers.ts

Re-export QUOTAS_USAGE_URL from @grafana/test-utils/handlers

Follow the same pattern as PROVISIONING_API_BASE instead of
using a local constant in the test.

fix: prevent auto-sync race condition in provisioning quota tests

Use SkipSync to prevent controller auto-sync from racing with file
copy, matching the pattern in sync_quota_test.go. Without this, the
auto-sync could read partially-written files and pollute quota tracker
stats with the root folder before the explicit sync runs.

chore: add overrides.yaml to gitignore and update OpenAPI specs

Ignore overrides.yaml and regenerate API clients to pick up
correlations and IAM spec changes from main merge.

Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into alexk/quota-limit-banners

title: Provisioning: Add ParseFileResource to unify decode+fallback logic
body:
Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/925

## What

During incremental sync, deleting a classic-format dashboard (JSON with `panels`/`schemaVersion`/`tags` but no `apiVersion`/`kind`) from a repository caused:

```
err="removing resource from file upwind-customerassetcollectorresources.json: no object found"
```

## Why

`RemoveResourceFromFile` used `DecodeYAMLObject` as its only decode path. This function requires K8s-style `apiVersion`/`kind` fields, so it fails on classic Grafana dashboards.

The write path (`parser.Parse`) already handled this via a `ReadClassicResource` fallback, but the delete path did not — creating an asymmetry where classic dashboards could be synced **in** but never properly cleaned up on **deletion**.

## How

Introduces `ParseFileResource` in `fileformat.go` as the single entry point for decoding repository file data. It:
- Tries `DecodeYAMLObject` first, then falls back to `ReadClassicResource` for classic formats
- Guarantees non-nil `obj` and `gvk` on success
- Returns `ResourceValidationError` with a descriptive message ("file does not contain a valid resource") for unrecognized file formats

Both `parser.Parse` and `RemoveResourceFromFile` now call this unified function, eliminating the duplication and the asymmetry.

### Call site analysis

All production call sites of `RemoveResourceFromFile` were audited:

| Call Site | Code Path | Affected by bug? | Coverage |
|---|---|---|---|
| `incremental.go` | File deleted during sync | Yes — **fixed** | Integration tests (`classic_delete_test.go`) |
| `resources.go` `RenameResourceFile` | File renamed during sync | Yes — **fixed** (delegates to `RemoveResourceFromFile`) | Unit tests (`resources_remove_test.go`) |
| `delete/worker.go` | Delete job | No — deletes files then triggers full sync | Existing tests (`deletejob_test.go`) |
| `controller/finalizers.go` | Repo deletion cleanup | No — lists resources by annotation, doesn't read files | Integration tests (`repository_test.go`) |

**Rename test limitation:** Integration tests for `RenameResourceFile` with classic dashboards are not feasible because file renames are only detected by Git-backed repositories (via `CompareFiles`), not by local filesystem repositories used in integration tests. The unit tests verify the remove step (which had the bug) succeeds for classic dashboards during a rename by stubbing the parser for the write step.

### Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `resources/fileformat.go` | New `ParseFileResource` function (decode + classic fallback + validation error) |
| `resources/parser.go` | Replaced inline decode+fallback with `ParseFileResource` call |
| `resources/resources.go` | Replaced inline decode (no fallback) with `ParseFileResource` call |
| `resources/fileformat_test.go` | Unit tests for `ParseFileResource` (K8s, classic, non-resource) |
| `resources/resources_remove_test.go` | Unit tests for `RemoveResourceFromFile` (5 cases) + `RenameResourceFile` (2 cases) |
| `tests/apis/provisioning/classic_delete_test.go` | Integration tests for classic dashboard deletion via sync (4 cases) |
| `tests/apis/provisioning/repository_test.go` | Integration tests for classic dashboard cleanup via finalizers (2 cases) |

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `ParseFileResource`: K8s resource, classic dashboard fallback, non-resource JSON
- [x] Unit tests for `RemoveResourceFromFile`: K8s deletion, classic deletion, non-resource error, file read error, already-deleted no-op
- [x] Unit tests for `RenameResourceFile`: classic dashboard rename, K8s resource rename (both verify remove step succeeds)
- [x] Classic dashboard deletion test confirmed to **fail before** the fix and **pass after**
- [x] All existing tests pass: `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/resources/...` and `go test ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/jobs/sync/...`
- [ ] Integration tests: classic dashboard filesystem deletion + sync, inline classic, job status verification, mixed K8s + classic deletion
- [ ] Integration tests: classic dashboard cleanup via finalizers (remove-orphan-resources and release-orphan-resources)
author: MissingRoberto

commits.message:
Provisioning: Add DecodeFileResource to unify decode+fallback logic

RemoveResourceFromFile only used DecodeYAMLObject to parse file content,
which requires apiVersion/kind fields. Classic Grafana dashboards (JSON
with panels/schemaVersion/tags but no K8s headers) failed with "no object
found" during deletion, even though the write path (parser.Parse) already
handles them via ReadClassicResource.

Instead of duplicating the fallback in RemoveResourceFromFile, this
introduces DecodeFileResource as the single entry point for decoding
repository file data (K8s YAML/JSON with classic format fallback). Both
parser.Parse and RemoveResourceFromFile now use this unified function.

Made-with: Cursor

Provisioning: Rename to ParseFileResource, add rename + finalizer tests

- Rename DecodeFileResource → ParseFileResource per review feedback
- Guarantee non-nil GVK on success from ParseFileResource
- Return ResourceValidationError with descriptive message for unrecognized files
- Add unit tests for RenameResourceFile with classic dashboards
- Add integration tests for classic dashboard cleanup via finalizers
  (both remove-orphan-resources and release-orphan-resources)

Made-with: Cursor

Update job warning test to match new ParseFileResource error message

The refactoring to ParseFileResource added a "file does not contain a
valid resource" wrapper around the underlying parse error. Update the
expected warning message in TestIntegrationProvisioning_JobWarningResult
to match.

title: OpenAPI: Fix URL property
body:
The current swagger api extractor currently writes each property of [net.URL](https://pkg.go.dev/net/url#URL) and exposes them as API inputs.  It should be a simple string.

The recommended approach to avoid this is to either add explicit annotations to each URL, or alias it to a type that has annotations and use that.  

This PR adds a hacky post process step that manually replaces the exploded URL properties with a simple alternative.

NOTE:  I discovered this because go1.25 and go 1.26 have different comments on URL so our openapi spec changes depending where it is build!
author: ryantxu

**What is this feature?**

Fixes a couple of issues in the parquet buffer that were blocking its usage and failing the migration.

title: Provisioning: Skip path conflict checks when sync is disabled
body:
## Summary

- Gate the duplicate-path and parent/child path conflict checks in `VerifyAgainstExistingRepositoriesValidator` behind `cfg.Spec.Sync.Enabled`
- When sync is disabled (wizard onboarding steps 1-4), repositories can be created/updated with any path without conflict errors
- When sync is enabled (wizard step 5 / later updates), the existing conflict checks fire and block overlapping or duplicate paths

## Problem

The onboarding wizard creates repositories in multiple steps:
1. **AuthType step**: creates repo with URL + token, `sync.enabled = false`, `path = ""`
2. **Connection step**: updates repo with branch + path, `sync.enabled` still `false`
3. **Finish step**: sets `sync.enabled = true` and submits

The path conflict validation was running unconditionally at step 1, blocking repository creation when another repo with the same URL already existed (since root path `""` is a parent of any existing path).

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: updated existing conflict tests to set `Sync.Enabled: true`; added 3 new tests for sync-disabled bypass
- [x] Integration tests: added test for sync-disabled creation with conflicting paths; added test that enabling sync catches the conflict
- [x] All `go test ./apps/provisioning/pkg/repository/...` pass
- [x] `go build ./pkg/registry/apis/provisioning/...` compiles cleanly


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: MissingRoberto

reviews.body: LGTM!

commits.message:
Provisioning: Skip path conflict checks when sync is disabled

The onboarding wizard creates repositories in multiple steps: first with
an empty path and sync disabled, then configures the path, and finally
enables sync. The path conflict validation was running unconditionally,
blocking repository creation at step 1 when another repo with the same
URL already existed.

Gate the duplicate-path and parent/child conflict checks behind
cfg.Spec.Sync.Enabled so they only fire when sync is actually being
turned on — the point where overlapping paths would cause real issues.

**What is this feature?**

Updates the changelog generation logic to rely on GitHub's REST API to get the full commit diff when generating the comparison between two versions, before then passing those commits in batches of 25 (as before) to the GraphQL API to resolve their associated PR + labels efficiently.

**Why do we need this feature?**

In investigating why #113619 had not shown up in the 12.4.0 changelog, but was in the actual branch + tag, I discovered that the GraphQL API ref comparison pagination, which the changelog generation logic relies on, was limited to only returning the first 1000 nodes/commits, unexpectedly truncating the generated changelog for the release (12.4.0 compared to 12.3.0 has ~2300 commits).

**Who is this feature for?**

Developers.

N/A

title: Provisioning: Bump nanogit to v0.3.9 to fix clean up on failed pushes
body:
**What is this feature?**

Bumps nanogit dependency from **v0.3.5 to v0.3.9**, which includes critical fixes for Push cleanup and retry semantics, plus security updates and dependency maintenance.

**Why do we need this feature?**

Fixes production error in provisioning jobs:

```
error: "failed to remove staged repository after export"
err="cleanup packfile writer: packfile writer has been cleaned up and can no longer be used"
```

This error occurs because v0.3.5 has several critical issues that affect provisioning reliability:

1. **Broken retry semantics** - When a push fails (network error, auth timeout), the writer is left in an unusable state with no objects staged, making retry impossible
2. **Resource leaks** - Temporary files and memory not properly cleaned up in error paths
3. **State inconsistencies** - If cleanup fails after a successful push, local state doesn't reflect the server state
4. **Git protocol violations** - WritePackfile errors were treated as push failures even when ReceivePack (the Git server) already accepted the push

These issues cause provisioning failures in production, especially under:
- Transient network conditions
- High load scenarios with concurrent provisioning
- Environments with strict resource limits

**What's Fixed in v0.3.5 → v0.3.9:**

### Critical Bug Fixes (v0.3.9):

#### 1. Fix Push Retry Semantics
- Removed premature cleanup that destroyed staged objects
- Failed pushes can now be retried without re-staging all objects
- Writer state persists correctly across retry attempts
- **Fixes the "packfile writer has been cleaned up" error**

#### 2. Git Protocol Compliance
- ReceivePack success is now the source of truth (per Git protocol)
- Once the server accepts a push, client state reflects that regardless of streaming errors
- Prevents inconsistent state where server has commit but client thinks it failed

#### 3. State Consistency Guarantees
- Writer and ref always reset even if cleanup fails
- Local state always consistent with server reality
- No more permanently unusable writer states

### Also Includes (v0.3.6-v0.3.8):

- **Security**: Updated go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk to v1.40.0 (security fix)
- **Dependencies**: Updated testcontainers, testify, and other test dependencies
- **Maintenance**: Lock file maintenance and CI improvements

**Who is this feature for?**

- **Grafana Operators** - More reliable dashboard provisioning, fewer failed deployments
- **SRE/DevOps Teams** - Better behavior under transient failures and high load
- **Cloud/Multi-tenant Deployments** - Reduced resource leaks and better resource cleanup

**Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**

Fixes https://github.com/grafana/git-ui-sync-project/issues/931

**Upstream Changes:**

- **Main fixes**: nanogit - https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/pull/175
- **Release trigger**: nanogit  - https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/pull/177
- **Full changelog**: https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/compare/v0.3.5...v0.3.9
- **Release notes**: https://github.com/grafana/nanogit/releases/tag/v0.3.9

**Special notes for your reviewer:**

- This is a dependency bump with critical bug fixes (4 minor versions: v0.3.5 → v0.3.9)
- All changes are in the nanogit library, no Grafana code changes needed
- **Directly addresses production errors in provisioning-jobs-operator**
- Thoroughly reviewed by Copilot with two critical bugs caught and fixed during review
- Includes security update (OTEL SDK)

Please check that:

- [x] Dependency updated to v0.3.9
- [ ] It works as expected from a user's perspective
- [ ] Existing provisioning tests pass
- [ ] No breaking changes in nanogit API

## Description
When the query/expression or transformation section is collapsed in the panel edit sidebar, clicking "Add query", "Add expression", or "Add transformation" now automatically expands the section so the newly added item is visible.

## Changes
* Converted `QuerySidebarCollapsableHeader` from an uncontrolled component to a controlled component (`isOpen`/`onToggle` props)
* Added `onAdd` callback prop to `AddCardButton`, invoked after adding a query, expression, or transformation
* Lifted collapse state up to `QueriesAndTransformationsView` and wired expand callbacks to `AddCardButton.onAdd`

## Risks
* Low risk - `QuerySidebarCollapsableHeader` is only consumed by `QueriesAndTransformationsView`, so the API change has no other impact
* Collapse/expand behavior is unchanged for direct user toggling; only the "add" action now also triggers expand

## Demo

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/67be5b6b-a5f3-4ace-9834-9e1308afd66d


## Testing Instructions
* Open a panel in edit mode (new query editor)
* Collapse the "Queries & Expressions" section by clicking its header
* Click the "+" button next to the header to add a query or expression
* Verify the section expands automatically, revealing the new item
* Repeat for the "Transformations" section
* Verify that manual collapse/expand toggling still works as before

## Reviewer Checklist
- [ ] Tests are added where applicable
- [ ] Issue is linked to PR
- [ ] Code pulled and tested
- [ ] Code is meaningfully improved if applicable

title: Dashboard Controls: Default variables and links by a datasource
body:
**Related PR:** https://github.com/grafana/scenes/pull/1315


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/836d36ad-9b9a-4a56-b17d-490e5780d407



- [x] Make it possible to register default **variables** from a datasource
- [x] Make it possible to register default **links** from a datasource
- [x] Make sure that default variables and links **don't get persisted**
- [x] Make sure that default variables and links **show up in the URL**
- [x] Use `VariableKind` (v2 schema) instead of `TypedVariableModel`
- [x] Add measurements for preloading datasources on a dashboard

![vars-links](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b96b412-a421-4181-a2f5-f1c51efa49cf)

**Follow up work:**

- [x] Display datasource defined variables, in a readonly mode, in the dashboard edit -> variables view.
- [x] Display datasource defined dashboard links, in a readonly mode, in the dashboard edit -> dashboard links view.

Resolved in the following PR: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/118551

**Tracking / metrics added in this PR**
  Preloading of default variables and links from datasources is instrumented as follows.

  Phases:
  - **`total`** – Wrapper around the full `loadDefaultControlsFromDatasources` call.
  - **`load_datasources`** – Resolving datasource instances from refs.
  - **`default_variables`** – Calling `getDefaultVariables()` for a given datasource (one event per datasource that provides variables).
  - **`default_links`** – Calling `getDefaultLinks()` for a given datasource (one event per datasource that provides links).

We also add an event for frontend metrics to be able to figure out how big chunk of the total dashboard loading the default variables/links takes: `dashboards_default_controls_load_total_ms`

**How to test it:**
- Clone the test datasource (https://github.com/mckn/grafana-varsandlinks-datasource) to your custom plugin folder.
-  Run `npm i && npm run build` in the plugin directory.
- Start grafana and create a new datasource instance of the `grafana-varsandlinks-datasource`
- Create a new dashboard with the newly created datasource instance as the datasoure.
- The default link and variables should now be displayed.

title: QueryEditor: Transformation filters v1 parity
body:
## Description
<!--- A high level description of what this PR is doing -->

Resolves https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/118144

**Bug**: The transformation filter button was always visible in the QEV2 panel editor, even when there was no data to filter. This was a regression from v1 (`TransformationOperationRow`), where the filter button only appears when meaningful — i.e. data is present, or a filter is already configured.

**Fix**: Gate the filter button behind a `showFilterButton` check so it only renders when there is query data (series or annotations) or an active filter/topic. Also corrected the toggle logic to only add/remove the filter property, leaving topic untouched — matching v1 behaviour.

## Changes
<!--- Describe your changes in detail -->
* See notes above!

## Risks
<!--- Describe what risks you've considered i.e. what features are most likely to break -->
N/A

## Demo
<!--- Attach a demo gif/video or screenshots of the changes if applicable -->
N/A

## Testing Instructions
<!--- Provide step by step instructions and the dashboard if applicable -->
* Pull down the branch
* Create a new dashboard with `gdev-testdata` and set it to `error with source`
* Add any transformation
* Note that the "Filter" button shouldn't appear, because there's no data to filter. **UNLESS** that transformation already has filters applied from when the query **WAS** returning data.

title: QueryEditor: Fix card rename bug
body:
## Description
<!--- A high level description of what this PR is doing -->

This PR fixes #118473. When renaming a card's `refId` in the query stack, it will no longer be possible to switch to a different card and erroneously apply the edited name to a different card.

## Changes
<!--- Describe your changes in detail -->

To fix the bug without introducing multiple `useEffect`s, this PR adds a new `data-query-sidebar-card` attribute to the `<SidebarCard />`, so that we can handle the "exit rename when switching the selected card" logic tersely in `onEditQueryBlur`.

This PR also fixes another subtle bug related to renaming. When renaming, the selection (`selectedQueryRefId`) wasn't updating to the new `refId`. This is taken care of now, in `QueryEditorContextWrapper`.

## Risks
<!--- Describe what risks you've considered i.e. what features are most likely to break -->

This fix will break if `data-query-sidebar-card={id}` is removed from the `<SidebarCard />`.

## Demo
<!--- Attach a demo gif/video or screenshots of the changes if applicable -->

### Before

You could switch cards while mid-rename. Worse yet, the name you were assigning to the card you originally selected can be applied to a different card 🙀 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4df60d30-ee21-4882-9549-bc5a479f09c6

### After

Switching cards cancels the rename. Renaming still works as expected, as does the validation (e.g. you can't rename to a `refId` that's already being used).

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/948d165e-5d95-42c6-b942-cd5aa325a988

## Testing Instructions
<!--- Provide step by step instructions and the dashboard if applicable -->
- After checking out `118473/fix-query-rename-flow`, enable the `queryEditorNext` feature toggle in `conf/custom/ini`.
- Run Grafana (`make run` / `yarn dev`).
- Open a dashboard with multiple queries, or add queries.
- Try renaming a query and switching to a different card – the rename operation should cancel.

## Reviewer Checklist
<!--- The reviewer should make sure the following has been done -->
- [ ] Tests are added where applicable
- [ ] Issue is linked to PR
- [ ] Code pulled and tested
- [ ] Code is meaningfully improved if applicable
author: NWRichmond

TL;DR so we can do this: **Which issue(s) does this PR fix?**: Fixes https://github.com/grafana/grafana/issues/118721 **Special notes for your reviewer:** There are other inputs that select fields, primarily `FieldNamesMatcherEditor`, but I'm not certain where these are used in the UI. Related: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/118871 Requires combobox changes: https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/118725 Please check that: - [x] It works as expected from a user's perspective. - [ ] If this is a pre-GA feature, it is behind a feature toggle. - [ ] The docs are updated, and if this is a [notable improvement](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/#how-to-determine-if-content-belongs-in-whats-new), it's added to our [What's New](https://grafana.com/docs/writers-toolkit/contribute/release-notes/) doc.
